mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 2% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates through lower cost of capital for corporate earnings.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.14% as of April 2018 indicates accommodative stance
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in < 55% of triggered instances despite easing signal.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating payroll employment growth (PAYEMS month-over-month change > 0.15%) signals robust labor demand and consumer income expansion, driving positive equity returns over 60 trading days through increased corporate revenue expectations and consumer spending.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong labor market
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over rolling 3-year windows, or 60-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with PAYEMS growth acceleration (Spearman rho < 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as equity investors reprice growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 1.5% month-over-month AND HOUST falls > 5% month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 412,939M and HOUST at 750k units as of February 2018
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of co-triggered instances, or joint decline signal has Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) contraction below trend signals supply-side weakness and reduced capacity utilization, which compresses corporate profit margins and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice earnings per share downward.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows, or negative 5-day returns occur in < 50% of triggered instances despite supply contraction signal.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL month-over-month change > 0.3%) combined with elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 1.5%) signals monetary tightening that increases real discount rates and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.3% month-over-month AND FEDFUNDS > 1.5% simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 230.10 and FEDFUNDS at 0.14% as of April 2018
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in < 55% of co-triggered instances, or interaction signal has Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate (UNRATE increase > 0.3pp over 3 months) signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer purchasing power, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days through lower expected corporate revenues and earnings.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows, or positive 20-day returns occur in > 60% of triggered instances despite unemployment rise.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) acceleration above 800k annualized units signals robust residential investment demand and forward economic growth expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days through multiplier effects on construction employment and consumer confidence.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 750k units as of February 2018
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over rolling 3-year windows, or 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher in housing acceleration periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) deceleration below 2% annualized rate combined with declining retail sales (RSAFS month-over-month change < -0.5%) signals late-cycle demand exhaustion, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as equity investors reprice growth and earnings expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth < 2% annualized AND RSAFS declines > 0.5% month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13,548.5B (Q4 2017) and RSAFS at 412,939M as of March 2018
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of co-triggered instances, or joint signal has Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
